Q6 NAT Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE • +1 / -0
Not attempted

A full 4-ary tree has 9 non-leaf nodes. How many leaf nodes does it have?

NAT
Correct answer: 28
Solution

28
Every leaf node converted into non-leaf node generate 3 extra leaf node.
The number of leaf node = 3 × (Internal nodes) + 1
= 3 × (9) + 1 = 28

Q7 MCQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Consider a string str, declared as “MADEEASYCSE”. Assuming that the size of a character is 1 bytes, choose the most appropriate choice.

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
strlen(str) counts the length of string, thus strlen( ) excludes the null character, unlike sizeof ( ) operator, which includes the null character.
Hence, the correct choice is option (c).

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)

Q12 MCQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Consider the following statements:
S1 : In an AVL tree, if the closest leaf to the root is at level k, then the node at level k – 1 (its parent) may have 1 child.
S2 : In an AVL tree, accepted balance factor for every node is 0, 1, –1 only.
Which of the above is correct?

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
Node at level k – 1 is allowed to have a single child that is the leaf node at level k.
So, S2 is true, node level k – 1 may have either 1 child or both the children.
In an AVL tree, accepted balance factor for every node is 0, 1, –1 only, so statement S2 should be
true.

Q14 MCQ Not checked

Consider the following code:

Assume Node is the structure type with two members : ‘value’ and ‘next’, identify the node value printed by the above code if non-empty linked list header is passed to the function find?

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
P1 traverses by node. P2 traverses by skipping one node. The node pointed by P1 is the middlenode in the linked list when P2 reach to NULL.
∴ Middle element of the list is printed by P1 → value.

Q24 MCQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Which of the following is true about linked list implementation of queue?
S1 : In enqueue operation. If new node is inserted at beginning of linked list, then in dequeue operation must be removed from end.
S2 : In enqueue operation. If new node is inserted at the end, then in dequeue operation node
must be removed from the beginning.

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
To keep the first in first out order a queue can be implemented using linked list in 2 ways.
1. If a new node is inserted at the beginning of linked list, then in dequeue operation node must be removed from end.
2. If new node is inserted at the end of linked list then, in dequeue operation, node must be removed from beginning.

Q30 MCQ Not checked

Not attempted

Suppose that queue operation are implemented using stack operation and dequeue( ) are queue operations where as pop( ) and push(x) are stack operations.
Consider the following code:

Fill the missing statement X1, X2 and X3 to perform dequeue operation correctly (here S1 and S2 are two stacks).

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
If S2 stack empty and S1 stack is not empty then we have to pop the element from stack S1 and push that element into stack S2 and return the stack S2 which contain newly inserted element.
